agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive and consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a shorter Highlights version. This report summarizes the city's finances, services, and achievements for the past year. It is a proposed presentation to Council — no decisions or new rules are being voted on at this time. The report is for information and discussion only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 and fee changes effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council approved repealing the Development Procedures Bylaw 2022 and replacing it with the Development Procedures Bylaw 2026, effective July 1, 2026. The new bylaw consolidates all development application procedures, introduces new development permit types, and updates fees in the Building Bylaw and Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Transition rules apply for applications in progress.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the new fee schedule and procedural requirements before submitting any development or building permit application after July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider endorsing DAP Program Phase 3 and applying for a 2026 award
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. This program aims to make the city's development approval system faster and more efficient. If endorsed, the city would also apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards, which recognize innovative local government projects. The decision is still upcoming — nothing has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for mobile devices and data plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, service plans, and data services. If approved, this contract would set the terms for purchasing phones, tablets, and wireless plans for city staff. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on aeration and overseeding contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. If approved, this would mean the city hires a company to aerate (poke small holes in the soil to let air, water, and nutrients reach the roots) and overseed (spread new grass seed over existing lawns) on city-owned land. This is a routine maintenance contract, not a new bylaw or regulation. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. upgrades from Patterson to Jubilee
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for upgrades to Victory Street between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Road. This proposed project would involve road improvements in that section of Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on the contract award at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on sidewalk contract for Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, Madison
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for a sidewalk construction project on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. If approved, this would mean new sidewalks will be built in those areas. The decision is on the upcoming council agenda and has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby's Sidewalk Expansion Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for the Sidewalk Expansion Program (EB-8022). If approved, this change would alter the terms of the contract used to build new sidewalks across the city. The decision is not final — Council will discuss and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the regional growth plan, Metro 2050. One change is proposed by the City of Maple Ridge for land near North 256 Street, and the other by the City of Surrey for the Hazelmere area. These are site-specific amendments that would change how the land can be used. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters. This is a proposal, not a final decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to review city street width standards
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a motion to direct city staff to review Burnaby's street width standards. This means staff would look at current rules for how wide streets must be built in new developments. If approved, this review could lead to changes in future street design requirements, which may affect how new subdivisions, roads, and related infrastructure are planned and built. The goal is to see if current standards still make sense for the city's needs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the review could affect future development costs and street design requirements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 2026 consultant budget cuts list
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal that would direct staff to prepare a detailed list of consultant budget cuts for 2026. If approved, staff would identify which consultant contracts to cut, which departments are affected, and which consultants would keep their funding. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. The goal is to reduce spending on outside consultants across city departments.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, it could affect city contracts your business holds.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider new rules for Burnaby Mountain trails
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This plan would set new rules for how trails on Burnaby Mountain are used, maintained, and protected. If approved, it could affect businesses that offer guided hikes, mountain biking tours, or other outdoor recreation on the mountain. The plan is still a draft — Council has not made a final decision yet. They are asking for public input before voting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you may want to comment if your business uses Burnaby Mountain trails.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's Bingham Cup 2028 bid
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from the Vancouver Roques Rugby club to support their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is a major international rugby tournament for gay and inclusive teams. Council's support would mean the City backs the bid, which could bring visitors and economic activity to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. Non-market housing means homes that are rented or sold at below-market rates, often run by a non-profit or government agency, to make them more affordable. This is an update on the project, which has not yet been approved. Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this project could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review status update on Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby for April 2026. This is a routine report to keep council informed about how major city projects are progressing. No decisions or new rules are being proposed at this time — it is an information item only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on major engineering projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on major engineering projects for the period of December 2025 to April 2026. This is a report to keep Council informed about the progress of large infrastructure projects. No decisions or bylaw changes are being proposed at this time — it is an information item only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - DECEMBER 2025 - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider status update on IT initiatives for early 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on the city's IT initiatives for January to March 2026. This is a report to keep council informed about ongoing technology projects. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time. The update is for information only and does not require a vote on new bylaws or policies.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES - JANUARY TO MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the IT initiatives for January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider Langara College study on inclusion in public recreation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive an update on a research project led by Langara College that looks at how to make public recreation programs and facilities in Burnaby more inclusive, diverse, equitable, and accessible. The project is still in progress, and no decisions have been made yet. The findings could lead to future changes in how recreation services are offered to better serve Burnaby’s diverse communities.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
PRCC - INCLUSION, DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND ACCESSIBILITY (IDEAS) IN PUBLIC RECREATION RESEARCH IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College, outlining its purpose, partnership model, and progress to date, while sharing emerging insights and demonstrating how the work supports improved access, inclusion, and participation in public recreation for Burnaby’s diverse communities.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Community Safety and Well-Being Plan update for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan, which outlines proposed actions to improve safety and well-being in Burnaby. The report includes a 2026 update on progress. This is not a final decision — Council is set to review and discuss the plan at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the plan could affect how the city allocates resources for community safety programs, but no new rules or fees are proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
PSC - SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on Burnaby Fire Department to February 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a status update on the Burnaby Fire Department covering operations up to the end of February 2026. This is a report for information only — no bylaw or policy change is being voted on at this time. The update will give Council a snapshot of the department's recent activities, response times, and any emerging issues. Business owners should be aware that this is a routine update, not a proposed new rule or fee.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7
PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on RCMP operations and annual performance plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a status update on RCMP policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for October to December 2025. This is an information item only — no vote or bylaw is proposed at this time. The report gives Council a look at recent police activities and how the RCMP is performing against its annual goals. Business owners should be aware that this is a routine update, not a new policy or regulation that would directly affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8
PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed new zoning bylaw that would replace the current 1965 version. The proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 would repeal the old rules and introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and new zoning district assignments. This is a major rewrite of the city's land-use rules. If approved, it will change what you can build, where you can build it, and what permits you need. The decision has not been made yet — council is still considering it.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to tell council how the new rules would affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update penalty rules for new Zoning Bylaw 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the city's penalty and enforcement rules. The change would remove old references to the 1965 Zoning Bylaw and replace them with the new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This means if you violate the new zoning rules, the city would use the updated Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to issue tickets and fines. The proposal does not change the penalties themselves — it just makes sure the enforcement system points to the correct, current zoning bylaw.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now — watch for council's decision and the final effective date of the new Zoning Bylaw 2026.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on new affordable housing definitions
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the official definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the city's Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw 2024. These charges are fees developers pay to the city to help fund community amenities like parks and infrastructure. If approved, the new definitions could affect which housing projects qualify for fee reductions or waivers, potentially impacting the cost and feasibility of future development in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development costs and project eligibility for fee reductions.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment impacts
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026. This bylaw would create new protections for tenants who are displaced when their building is redeveloped. The proposal is still in the early stages — it has received first, second, and third readings, but it has not been finally approved or enacted yet. If passed, it would set rules for how landlords must treat tenants during redevelopment projects.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council vote — this bylaw could affect your property if you plan to redevelop. No public hearing is scheduled yet.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This change is meant to improve tenant protection. If approved, the amendment would update how the city enforces rules related to rental housing, making it easier to hold landlords accountable for violations. The exact details of the new enforcement measures are not yet public, but the goal is to give tenants stronger legal backing when their rights are violated. This is a proposed change only — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this could affect how tenant complaints are handled in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby mid-year fee and charge updates for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the City of Burnaby's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for the middle of 2026. This means fees for various city services, permits, and licences could change. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed fee changes — they could affect your business licence or permit costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and scooter pilot program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw amendment that would allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters on Burnaby streets. A proposed bylaw would change the existing Street and Traffic Bylaw to permit these devices in a controlled trial. This is not yet law — council will decide at an upcoming meeting whether to approve the pilot.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect how you operate your business or use e-bikes/scooters in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw amendment that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The proposed change to the Parks Regulation Bylaw would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. If approved, the pilot would run for a set period to test the impact on park users and safety.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, review the new park rules before riding an e-bike or scooter in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to support a new pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this change would allow the city to enforce rules for these devices under the existing ticketing system. The pilot program is still being developed, and this amendment is a step toward making it official. Business owners who sell, rent, or service e-bikes and e-scooters should watch for updates, as the pilot could affect how these vehicles are used on city streets.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how e-bikes and e-scooters are regulated in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in the city for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set property tax rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you will pay next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your property tax bill for 2026.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on abandoning rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to abandon a rezoning bylaw for 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858/9898 Gatineau Place. The original rezoning, approved in September 2024, allowed two residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) with a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant now wants to remove the office space and add 292 more market strata residential units instead. Council will decide whether to scrap the old plan and let the applicant submit a new one.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, a new rezoning application will follow that could affect nearby property values and development.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
